meta_title: AIRadar — A Living Map of India's AI Startup Ecosystem | AIBoomi
hero_title: A living map of India's *AI startup ecosystem.*
hero_lede1: AIRadar tracks and celebrates the AI-native founders, startups, and movements reshaping India's future — an always-on radar for the ecosystem's quiet builders.
hero_lede2: **AIRadarDaily**, our flagship series, profiles one AI-native startup a day, Monday to Friday — live since 26 January 2026, Republic Day. Not noise. Not hype. Just signal.
about_1_body: This is our way of paying it forward to the founders making India a global AI powerhouse. Every story is a signal that world-class AI products are rising from India — not just Indiranagar and Koramangala, but Surat, Lucknow, and beyond.
about_2_body: India isn't just building "wrappers". We are building the infrastructure, the models, and the vertical applications for the world. From vibe coding to voice AI, textile design to enterprise memory — the range speaks for itself.
about_3_body: We feature builders across LinkedIn, X, and Substack. No pitch decks needed — just tell us what you're building, why it matters, and why you. Write to [airadar@aiboomi.org](mailto:airadar@aiboomi.org)
mile_eyebrow: // MILESTONE_100 · A NOTE FROM AVINASH RAGHAVA
mile_quote: I started a small experiment. Every morning, my team and I would sit down and write about one AI-native startup from India. ==No rankings, awards, fundraising filters, or popularity contests. Just founders building.==
mile_p1: Over these 100 days, I had a front-row seat to how founders across India are thinking about the future. Across 100 companies, I saw different answers — but there was one common thread: the most compelling founders weren't fascinated by AI for its own sake. They were obsessed with solving important problems.
mile_p2: Many of the founders we featured are still relatively unknown. They aren't dominating social feeds or speaking at every conference. Yet day after day, they kept appearing on our radar. Building. Shipping. Learning. Iterating. Moving forward without waiting for attention or validation.
mile_p3: #AIRadarDaily was never a content initiative. Communities have a responsibility to shine a light beyond the obvious — to help exceptional founders become visible before the market discovers them, and to document an ecosystem while it is still being shaped.
mile_p4: When I look at these logos, I don't see a collection of companies. I see founder journeys, conviction, resilience, and teams choosing to build at a moment when the rules of technology are being rewritten in real time. India's AI story is still in its earliest chapters. The energy is real. The talent is undeniable.
mile_p5: None of this happens by accident. My special thanks to [Jay Ingle](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jayingle/), who curates and operates AIRadar for our ecosystem — showing up every single day with the same quiet consistency as the builders we feature. His relentless support made these stories possible, and keeps the radar on.
signals_sub: // {N} AI-native startups, profiled one day at a time
band_body: We feature AI-native builders across LinkedIn, X, and Substack. No pitch decks needed — just tell us what you're building, why it matters, and why you.
footer_line: AIRadar is an [AIBoomi](https://aiboomi.org) initiative · Curated & Co-Authored by [Jay Ingle](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jayingle/) · Let's celebrate the builders.
